jokes.text: First of all, I'd like to say I-I personally didn't think any of us would be standing in front of you here today. No, no, I mean... Shit, no. I thought we were going straight to hell.
jokes.setup: Charlie starts what sounds like a sincere address to religious authority
jokes.punchline: Reveals his low expectations were about literally going to hell, not metaphorically
jokes.explanation: Strong opening that establishes the stakes and Charlie's characteristic bluntness. Good subversion of expectations.

jokes.text: I did what I did. You don't like it, you can kiss my ass.
jokes.setup: Charlie seems to be showing remorse
jokes.punchline: Immediately becomes defiant and crude to apparent religious authority
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie inappropriateness at the worst possible moment

jokes.text: Hey, could we be judged individually? 'Cause I don't want to be lumped in with him.
jokes.setup: Charlie just said something inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately tries to distance himself, showing his self-preservation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis move - self-serving and cowardly while trying to sound reasonable

jokes.text: Goddamn, this ship is the tits! Frank, Frank, watch your language. You know? This is a Christian cruise.
jokes.setup: Frank enthusiastically praises the ship with crude language
jokes.punchline: Mac immediately scolds him about language while they're supposedly being devout
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition showing Mac's selective devotion and Frank's inability to filter himself

jokes.text: Oh, I didn't find them. They found me. And it's all, of course, a part of God's plan.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Mac finding a church
jokes.punchline: Mac claims divine intervention in what's clearly just winning a raffle
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac religious delusion - finding deep meaning in mundane coincidence

jokes.text: Hey, Mac, is God gonna be able to protect against the ship sinking? God protects all things, Charlie. He is an awesome god. The only way this ship is gonna sink is if the captain's drunk.
jokes.setup: Charlie asks if God will protect the ship
jokes.punchline: Mac's absolute faith immediately followed by a very specific earthly concern
jokes.explanation: Great foreshadowing and irony - Mac's faith paired with practical worry creates comedy and tension

jokes.text: we will each have to have a half a cap of mouthwash every day. You know, just to stave off the shakes.
jokes.setup: Discussion about the dry cruise and God's rules
jokes.punchline: Dennis reveals they're planning to drink mouthwash for alcohol, treating withdrawal like a medical necessity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy showing their alcoholism while maintaining the pretense of religious devotion

jokes.text: You're supposed to say, 'And also with you.' Why is that? I'm not gonna say that.
jokes.setup: Mac expects the traditional religious response
jokes.punchline: Dennis flatly refuses to participate in basic religious courtesy
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's reluctance to commit to even the most basic religious participation

jokes.text: I'm gonna be the captain. Charlie's gonna be the first mate. No, no, no, I-I'm putting a stop to this right now. No costumes, no high jinks.
jokes.setup: Frank announces costume plans
jokes.punchline: Dee tries to be the voice of reason, which is inherently absurd
jokes.explanation: Sets up the dynamic of Dee trying unsuccessfully to be the mature one

jokes.text: can we take a vacation from our usual bullshit? So don't be gluttonous. No wrath. No lust for me.
jokes.setup: Dee wants them to avoid their usual behavior
jokes.punchline: She lists the seven deadly sins as if they're their normal operating procedures
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-awareness joke - their 'usual bullshit' is literally the deadly sins

jokes.text: which is gonna be difficult, given the implications of being on a boat.
jokes.setup: Dennis says he'll avoid lust
jokes.punchline: Immediately references his predatory 'implication' theory about boats
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Dennis can't help but reveal his sociopathic nature even when trying to be good

jokes.text: I got to take the cross off that Mac gave us. This thing is burning me to shit.
jokes.setup: Frank mentions removing his cross necklace
jokes.punchline: The cross is literally burning him, suggesting he might be evil/demonic
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag setup that plays on vampire/demon tropes with Frank as the evil one

jokes.text: Oh! Goddamn, Frank. Oh, my God. You might actually be the devil.
jokes.setup: Frank removes the cross
jokes.punchline: The visual reveal of what the cross did to Frank's chest shocks everyone
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the setup, with strong visual comedy and character reactions

jokes.text: I could be Satan. Screw God! (thunderous horn blows)
jokes.setup: Frank realizes he might be evil
jokes.punchline: He immediately embraces it and blasphemes, followed by ominous sound
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and timing - Frank goes full evil immediately, with perfect sound effect timing

jokes.text: That being said, the timing of that big boat noise was... pretty suspect, so, Frank, maybe don't push his buttons?
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to be rational about the horn
jokes.punchline: But admits the timing was suspicious and suggests not angering God
jokes.explanation: Great Dennis moment - trying to be logical while clearly being spooked

jokes.text: I should have taken on a missionary position with them.
jokes.setup: Mac talks about helping his friends find salvation
jokes.punchline: Uses 'missionary position' to mean religious work, creating sexual double entendre
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that works on Mac's religious earnestness and sexual naivety

jokes.text: I even started putting battery acid on the back of his cross necklace to burn through his skin. Why? Oh, to subtly send the message that he might be a demon.
jokes.setup: Mac explains his methods for helping Frank
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been poisoning Frank with battery acid as a 'subtle' religious message
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Mac's idea of 'subtle' is literally poisoning someone, reveals his dangerous religious extremism

jokes.text: Oh, well, yikes.
jokes.setup: Mac just described poisoning someone with battery acid
jokes.punchline: David's mild, understated reaction to hearing about assault with acid
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement - treating attempted poisoning as a minor social awkwardness

jokes.text: Fun? No, no, the songs aren't supposed to be fun, they're supposed to be bleak and dreary, so we can focus on how bad we are.
jokes.setup: Christians suggest their songs are fun
jokes.punchline: Mac insists religious music should be miserable and self-flagellating
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing Mac's twisted understanding of Christianity as purely punitive

jokes.text: We need to punish our ears to pay for the original sin of being born.
jokes.setup: Mac continues explaining proper Christian worship
jokes.punchline: Takes Catholic guilt to an extreme where existence itself requires punishment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Mac's religious extremism - even being born deserves punishment

jokes.text: I feel like I've been waiting for this my entire life. We're glad you had fun. Yes, yeah. I'm ready to go again. Well, you've already gone twice. We're sort of taking turns...
jokes.setup: Mac discovers he loves the musical number
jokes.punchline: Becomes obsessively enthusiastic while others try to politely limit his participation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Mac's social obliviousness and enthusiasm overwhelming others

jokes.text: These winter coats were a good idea-- it is chilly. They're cranking the air conditioning. You don't want to be sick on a boat.
jokes.setup: Frank and Charlie are wearing heavy coats
jokes.punchline: They're wearing winter coats in what's clearly not cold weather, rationalizing it
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing their poor judgment and tendency to over-rationalize bad decisions

jokes.text: Oh, no, Charlie. That's just orange juice. There's no alcohol. Just orange juice, like the mixer? Yeah. People drink it. That's crazy to me.
jokes.setup: Charlie thinks orange juice must contain alcohol
jokes.punchline: Can only conceptualize orange juice as a mixer, finds straight juice incomprehensible
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work showing their alcoholism - they can't imagine non-alcoholic beverages

jokes.text: Oh, I had a diet cola mixer a while ago. Was it any good? Yeah, oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Discussion of drinking straight mixers
jokes.punchline: Charlie fondly recalls drinking straight diet cola like it was a cocktail
jokes.explanation: Continues the alcoholic logic theme - treating soda like a sophisticated beverage choice

jokes.text: You can lie, but that burp told me the whole story.
jokes.setup: Frank denies drinking beer
jokes.punchline: Charlie reveals he detected beer through Frank's burp
jokes.explanation: Good detective work comedy and quotable line about involuntary evidence

jokes.text: Oh, this, uh, this is an empty. A pelican dropped it and we... Yeah, we picked it up.
jokes.setup: Caught with beer, need to explain it
jokes.punchline: Blame a pelican for dropping empty beer cans on the ship
jokes.explanation: Absurd excuse that's obviously false but delivered with conviction

jokes.text: Well, I don't want to run back and forth to the cabin all the time, it's so far away. Son of a bitch!
jokes.setup: Frank explains why he has so much beer
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's wearing multiple beer cans hidden in his coat
jokes.explanation: Great visual payoff showing Frank's practical but ridiculous problem-solving

jokes.text: That's my seat, bitch. Excuse me. Sorry. Got a little worked up. Uh, not quite in vacation mode yet. Let me try again. Um, please get your shit off my seat.
jokes.setup: Dee tries to claim a pool chair
jokes.punchline: Attempts to be polite but immediately returns to aggression
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Dee's inability to control her anger even when trying to be better

jokes.text: Well, there are certain 'see you next Tuesdays' on this ship that are making it very difficult.
jokes.setup: Dennis talks about controlling his wrath
jokes.punchline: Uses euphemism 'see you next Tuesdays' (C-U-N-T) to insult people while sounding polite
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Dennis trying to be refined while being crude

jokes.text: See this, uh, flaxen-haired seductress across the pool over here? Dennis, she looks like she's 12 years old. No, she's of age. I checked.
jokes.setup: Dennis points out an attractive woman
jokes.punchline: Dee's concern about age and Dennis's disturbing clarification that he 'checked'
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy showing Dennis's predatory nature and the disturbing implication he investigates women's ages

jokes.text: because of the implication. Not that you would understand. It's not what you think it is.
jokes.setup: Dennis mentions how easy it would be to have the woman
jokes.punchline: References his famous 'implication' theory about women being unable to refuse on boats
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to one of the show's most famous dark comedy moments, shows Dennis can't help revealing his nature

jokes.text: No, I think I get it. We're out in the middle of the ocean. She's stuck on a boat. She couldn't possibly say no, 'cause something might go wrong for her if she did. That's... that's exactly what it is.
jokes.setup: Dee says she doesn't understand the implication
jokes.punchline: Perfectly explains Dennis's predatory logic, shocking him with her understanding
jokes.explanation: Brilliant moment where Dee perfectly articulates Dennis's sociopathic logic, horrifying him

jokes.text: How did you get that so fast?
jokes.setup: Dee perfectly explained his implication theory
jokes.punchline: Dennis is shocked that she understood his predatory logic so easily
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction showing Dennis's concern that his 'subtle' predatory behavior is transparent

jokes.text: It's like when I'm alone with a guy, and we're messing around, and he gets all skittish about banging. So then I insinuate that it would be a shame if my account of what happened was different from his and then he ended up getting a call from the sheriff.
jokes.setup: Dee explains how she understands Dennis's logic
jokes.punchline: Reveals she uses false sexual assault threats to coerce men
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation showing Dee is just as manipulative and predatory as Dennis, using false accusations

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Oh, a bird shit on me! A bird shit in my mouth! I'm literally getting shit on!
jokes.setup: Dee has been talking about not wanting to be 'shit on' metaphorically
jokes.punchline: Literally gets shit on by a bird, fulfilling the ironic prophecy
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic comeuppance - literal bird shit on the character nicknamed 'bird' who complains about being shit on

jokes.text: I brought my cat-o'-nine-tails. You know, so, flagellate me. Three-three lashes for missing.
jokes.setup: Mac wants punishment for missing the game
jokes.punchline: Brought a medieval torture device for missing shuffleboard on a cruise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Mac's religious extremism - bringing medieval torture devices to a leisure cruise

jokes.text: Why would you want me to do that? So I could be a better boy.
jokes.setup: David asks why Mac wants to be whipped
jokes.punchline: Mac's childlike explanation for wanting physical punishment
jokes.explanation: Disturbing infantilization showing Mac's warped understanding of religion and morality

jokes.text: Well, sure, yeah, it's a wonderful day now, but look at that storm coming. That's impending doom right there. He probably sent that storm because I keep failing him.
jokes.setup: Christian tries to point out the beautiful day
jokes.punchline: Mac sees approaching storm as divine punishment for his shuffleboard performance
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Mac's paranoid narcissism - weather is about his game performance

jokes.text: In your face! I beat your asses. Gay. Gay. Gay, gay, gay, gay.
jokes.setup: Mac succeeds at shuffleboard and celebrates
jokes.punchline: Immediately calls thunder 'gay' while surrounded by gay Christians
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing Mac's casual homophobia while trying to fit in with gay Christians

jokes.text: What are you doing? Why are you two kissing? Uh, because he's my husband, and we love each other.
jokes.setup: Mac sees two men kissing and is confused
jokes.punchline: Simple, matter-of-fact explanation that they're married, highlighting Mac's bigotry
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective contrast between normal love and Mac's shocked reaction

jokes.text: You think thunder is God clapping?
jokes.setup: Mac claims God is responding to the gay kiss with thunder
jokes.punchline: Reasonable person questions Mac's insane interpretation of weather
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to Mac's ridiculous supernatural interpretation

jokes.text: I knew you couldn't stop from being gluttonous. That's gone, too. I kind of drank... all of it. Charlie drank it. He needed to calm down.
jokes.setup: Dee accuses them of gluttony, asks for mouthwash
jokes.punchline: Charlie drank all the mouthwash to calm his nerves
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing their alcoholism - even mouthwash isn't safe from Charlie's drinking

jokes.text: A virgin daiquiri for the virgin?
jokes.setup: Dennis considers sending a drink to the young woman
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'virgin' for both the drink and his assumption about the woman
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that reveals Dennis's creepy assumptions about the woman

jokes.text: There are two guys in this church... that are gay. Who's the other guy? I don't get the joke.
jokes.setup: Mac reports there are two gay men in the church
jokes.punchline: Others laugh because Mac is obviously the other gay man, but he doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony - everyone except Mac knows he's gay, making his homophobia tragic and funny

jokes.text: Well, guys, being gay is not Christian.
jokes.setup: Mac tries to explain why gay Christians are wrong
jokes.punchline: States his bigoted view to a group of gay Christians
jokes.explanation: Basic cringe moment showing Mac's ignorance and bigotry, but not particularly clever

jokes.text: That's the bitch who stole my chair! Magic? The dark arts? First the queers. Then sorcery? This cruise is going straight to hell.
jokes.setup: Magic show begins with the woman who took Dee's chair
jokes.punchline: Mac sees magic as witchcraft and evidence of moral decay
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Mac's religious paranoia and Dee's pettiness converging on one target

jokes.text: So we can go anywhere we want on the ship now. That's not exactly the first mate's costume I had in mind. Hey, man, you said 'first mate.' That's the only guy I know.
jokes.setup: Frank and Charlie are in costume to move around the ship
jokes.punchline: Charlie's interpretation of 'first mate' is apparently inappropriate/sexual
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about Charlie's misunderstanding of nautical terms with sexual implications

jokes.text: So, what? We're just gonna drink straight boat fuel? No. We're gonna cut it. With what? Mixers.
jokes.setup: Frank suggests drinking from the ship's fuel pipes
jokes.punchline: Plans to mix boat fuel with cocktail mixers like it's a reasonable drink recipe
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of their alcoholism - treating industrial fuel as cocktail ingredients

jokes.text: Well, that and the other church was getting super Vietnamese.
jokes.setup: Mac explains why he joined this church to get closer to God
jokes.punchline: Reveals casual racism as another motivation for switching churches
jokes.explanation: Casually reveals Mac's racism while he's supposedly on a spiritual journey

jokes.text: Now watch while I turn these spades into stars. Oh! Oh, shit! Who's getting shit on now, bitch?
jokes.setup: Dee attempts a magic trick that goes wrong
jokes.punchline: Cards explode in woman's face, Dee celebrates instead of apologizing
jokes.explanation: Good slapstick payoff with Dee's vindictive celebration of accidentally hurting someone

jokes.text: Well, what is this, boat jail? I'm not a goddamn Somali pirate, okay?
jokes.setup: Dee is being taken to the brig
jokes.punchline: Compares herself to Somali pirates as if that's the relevant comparison
jokes.explanation: Absurd comparison showing Dee's warped perspective on maritime crime

jokes.text: Fire the torpedoes! Full steam ahead!
jokes.setup: Frank is dressed as captain during a storm
jokes.punchline: Gives military naval commands during a civilian cruise emergency
jokes.explanation: Frank getting carried away with his costume during actual crisis

jokes.text: don't let him drink that! Don't let him drink our torpedo juice!
jokes.setup: Captain confiscates their boat fuel cocktail
jokes.punchline: Charlie calls the dangerous fuel mixture 'torpedo juice' like it's a legitimate cocktail
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their makeshift alcohol with naval terminology

jokes.text: I'm the daiquiri man, but I suspect you already knew that, didn't you?
jokes.setup: Dennis approaches the young woman he's been stalking
jokes.punchline: Assumes she's aware of his attention and calls himself 'daiquiri man'
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis narcissism - assuming his attention is noteworthy and welcome

jokes.text: The thunder's really... It's throwing this whole thing off. It's making everything I say seem sinister, which it's not intended to be, you know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Dennis realizes his words sound threatening
jokes.punchline: Blames the thunder instead of his actual sinister intentions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant moment where Dennis almost gains self-awareness but deflects blame to weather

jokes.text: 'Come in your room.' That's not what I meant. I would like to go in your room.
jokes.setup: Dennis realizes his phrasing sounded sexual
jokes.punchline: Tries to clarify but the correction is still threatening
jokes.explanation: Dennis trying to be less crude while maintaining the threat

jokes.text: I also feel like maybe... you wouldn't dare.
jokes.setup: Dennis suggests the woman might say no to his proposal
jokes.punchline: Explicitly states the implication that she can't refuse safely
jokes.explanation: The most explicit statement of Dennis's predatory implication theory - chilling perfection of character

jokes.text: It was a misunderstanding! The storm made it seem way worse than it is. She was never in any danger.
jokes.setup: Dennis is in jail for his behavior with the woman
jokes.punchline: Blames the storm for making his threatening behavior seem threatening
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis delusion - refusing to acknowledge his actual threatening behavior

jokes.text: You're soaking wet. Are you okay? Yeah. I was out in the rain looking for a sign from God.
jokes.setup: Mac arrives soaked from the storm
jokes.punchline: Was outside in dangerous weather hoping for divine communication
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's dangerous religious obsession

jokes.text: I'm here to convert you. From being gay. You want to convert us? You realize how insulting that is?
jokes.setup: Mac announces his purpose
jokes.punchline: Tries to convert gay Christians from being gay, showing complete social obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Peak Mac bigotry and social incompetence in one moment

jokes.text: There's no way you guys could convert me to your lifestyle. Let's give it a shot.
jokes.setup: Mac claims it's impossible to convert him to being gay
jokes.punchline: Gay man accepts the challenge, knowing Mac is already gay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony since everyone knows Mac is gay except Mac himself

jokes.text: Well... I'm gay. Yeah. No shit. Oh, you guys knew this already? That you were gay? From the day we met.
jokes.setup: Mac announces he's gay like it's news
jokes.punchline: Everyone immediately confirms they've always known
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to seasons of Mac being obviously gay while in denial

jokes.text: You let the door close behind you?! I couldn't find you guys. I wanted to hang out. I had something important to tell you. What is it? There is no God.
jokes.setup: Mac voluntarily entered the brig to share important news
jokes.punchline: His spiritual crisis revelation traps them all together
jokes.explanation: Great escalation where Mac's need to share bad news traps everyone

jokes.text: If there was a God, there's no way that he would make me queer, right?
jokes.setup: Mac explains his loss of faith
jokes.punchline: His logic for atheism is based on his own internalized homophobia
jokes.explanation: Tragic-comic moment where Mac's self-hatred leads to spiritual crisis

jokes.text: Honestly, what's the point anyway? There's no meaning to any of it. There's no God, there's no heaven, there's no hell. Wrong!
jokes.setup: Mac spirals into nihilistic despair
jokes.punchline: Dee violently disagrees about the existence of hell
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Dee's hell revelation that's coming

jokes.text: You know where hell is, Mac? You know where hell is? It's right here. It's right now. We are in hell!
jokes.setup: Dee wants to escape her friends but can't
jokes.punchline: Realizes their eternal punishment is being trapped together
jokes.explanation: Brilliant existential revelation that recontextualizes the entire episode and series - their punishment is each other
